posts.name: craig v
posts.cooked:
Who makes (your favorite ) basic bourgogne rouge?

  • 1. Hubert Lignier
  • 2. Chevillon
  • 3. Meo Cumzet
  • 4. Forey
  • 5. Hudelot Noellat
  • 6. J&L Trapet
  • 7. Jean Cluade Rammonet
  • 8. Leroy ( not priced like most)
  • 9. A&P de Villaine
  • 10. Michelle Lafarge
  • 11. Joseph Viollot
  • 12. Jane et Sylvain
  • 13. Fourrier
  • 14. Michel Gaunoux
  • 15. Domaine Montille
  • 16. Ghislaine Barthod
  • 17. Bruno Clavelier
  • 18. Faiveley
  • 19. Lamarche
  • 20. Roty

0 voters

Who makes your favorite Bourgogne rouge. I’ll list as many as I can think of to vote on. You tell us the ones I forgot and should have listed. With 2015 being a great vintage it usually a good time to explore the old saying “a rising tides lifts all boats” theory. Do you even drink Bourgogne rouge? I do and find them intriguing and pretty much always worth their sub $35 tariffs.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: In the absence of Mugneret-Gibourg, voted Barthod.

posts.name: Dale Williams
posts.cooked: I voted for Chevillon, just because I have more than other producers listed (or about same amount as Michel Lafarge), except Bachelet who was not listed. But could have easily voted for Meo-Camuzet, or others not listed like Camus-Bruchon. I enjoy them all, but really couldn’t choose a favorite.

posts.name: craig v
posts.cooked:
My favorite Chevillon to date has been the 08 which I wish I had more of. The 10 is fantastic too. I also own and drink 10,13 & 14 and got some 16s coming. Just had my first '15 and it was pretty closed.

Dale Williams:

I voted for Chevillon, just because I have more than other producers listed (or about same amount as Michel Lafarge), except Bachelet who was not listed. But could have easily voted for Meo-Camuzet, or others not listed like Camus-Bruchon. I enjoy them all, but really couldn’t choose a favorite.

posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked:
Yeah, no MG means flawed poll.

I picked Hudelot Noellat, but they are a distant second.

posts.name: Marcus Dean
posts.cooked:
TBurklow:

Arnoux Pinot Fin is delish

+1, it is quite yummy

Poll. Flawed.

Mugneret-Gibourg is the most obvious missing one, though it is not cheap (though not in Leroy territory, either).

I’m actually not sure how many of these are sub-$35. Forey is for sure where I am, and I do like the wines. Barthod is pricier (though still a buy).

Voillot (aka Viollot) might be my pick for best value in this very flawed poll.

posts.name: Gerhard P.  (P r @ € s e n t)
posts.cooked:

posts.name: craig v
posts.cooked:
It would be great if I had more than 20 options I could list. Polls will always have someone’s favorite option overlooked.

Mugneret-Gibourg is the most obvious missing one, though it is not cheap (though not in Leroy territory, either).

I’m actually not sure how many of these are sub-$35. Forey is for sure where I am, and I do like the wines. Barthod is pricier (though still a buy).

Voillot (aka Viollot) might be my pick for best value in this very flawed poll.

posts.name: Warren Taranow
posts.cooked: I like the Bertheau (also missing), Meo Camuzet, and Hudelot Noellert. I’ll have to try the M-G.

posts.name: Jonathan G
posts.cooked: Out of the ones I’ve tried, Hudelot-Noellat has been my favourite.
